prompt.body:
**Source:** technical-RFC slice B (epic #1). One task = one PR; a dev implements from this Issue alone.
Follows A1 (#3) + A2 (#5), both merged — the skill now owns the living documentation model at
`skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md`.

## Goal

Every in-repo file that still names the vault `01-conventions` as the **living** documentation model is
repointed to the skill's `skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md`, and a fail-closed
`check_model_refs` scan (with a test that rides `check_cmd`) guards against any new living-model reference
to `01-conventions` creeping back in.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] No in-repo file names the vault `01-conventions` as the *living* documentation model. The three
      living-model references are repointed to the skill's `skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md`:
      `AGENTS.md` (the "The documentation model itself is …" line), `templates/product-spec.md` (the
      "*Review output* … see … *Reviewing a doc*" pointer), and `templates/feature-rfc.md` (same pointer).
- [ ] A new scan tool lives at `tools/` (name it `check_model_refs.py`): given `--scan-root` (default: the
      repo root), it flags **every** `01-conventions` occurrence — both the bare name and its full
      vault-path form (iterations/3-upper-pipeline/01-conventions.md) — **except** occurrences in an explicit
      allowlist, printing one `<file>: <message>` line per un-allowlisted hit and exiting **1** if any remain,
      **0** otherwise (the argparse / exit-1 shape of `tools/check_links.py:121-137`).
- [ ] The scan skips VCS/build dirs (`.git`, `.venv`, `node_modules`) so the real-tree run is deterministic.
- [ ] The allowlist is explicit and **seeded** with today's legitimate mentions so the scan is green on this
      repo: the `documentation-model.md` origin note (lineage), the router test's absence-guard in
      `tests/test_skill_factory_router.py`, and the scanner + its own test (which contain the literal by
      construction). A **new** mention anywhere else fails loud until deliberately allowlisted.
- [ ] A test at `tests/` (name it `test_check_model_refs.py`) covers the tool: unit cases (an un-allowlisted
      hit → exit 1; the same hit allowlisted → exit 0) **and** a real-tree scan over the actual repo that
      asserts exit 0 — so the consumer-integrity guard rides `check_cmd` on every future build.
- [ ] `pytest tests/ -q` is green with the repo repointed and the tool + test added.

## Context & links

Self-contained slice (source: technical RFC on epic #1; A1 #3 + A2 #5 merged). Two moving parts: repoint the
stragglers, then add a standing guard so this can't regress.

**Repoint targets** (exact current text — the implementer reads the surrounding prose in the worktree and
keeps the sentence reading naturally; only the reference changes):
- `AGENTS.md` — the line "The documentation model itself is …/3-upper-pipeline/01-conventions.md" →
  name `skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md` (the factory skill) as the model instead.
- `templates/product-spec.md` — "*Review output:* … never a frozen appendix (see `01-conventions` →
  *Reviewing a doc*)." → point at the skill's `documentation-model` (its *Reviewing a doc* section).
- `templates/feature-rfc.md` — same "see `01-conventions` → *Reviewing a doc*" pointer → same repoint.

**Why a scan, not a hand-kept list** (the spec's release criterion): references added *before* release must
be caught too, so the guard is a scan over the tree, not a fixed list. It's fail-closed — any un-allowlisted
`01-conventions` occurrence exits 1.

**The tool** — `check_model_refs.py` under `tools/`. Copy the shape of `tools/check_links.py:121-137`:
`main(argv=None)` + argparse, one `print(f"{path}: {msg}")` per hit, `return 1 if errors else 0`, and
`sys.exit(main())` under `if __name__ == "__main__":`. It walks text files under `--scan-root` (default repo
root), skipping `.git` / `.venv` / `node_modules`, and reports every line containing `01-conventions`
(bare, or within the full vault path iterations/3-upper-pipeline/01-conventions.md) that isn't allowlisted.

**The allowlist** — an explicit in-module constant (or a sidecar text file) enumerating the legitimate
mentions. Seed it so the current tree scans clean:
- the origin note in `skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md` (line ~3: "Relocated from the vault
  `01-conventions` … which it supersedes" — lineage, must stay);
- the two absence-guard lines in `tests/test_skill_factory_router.py` (they assert `01-conventions` is *gone*
  from the router — legitimate);
- `check_model_refs.py` itself and `test_check_model_refs.py` (both contain the literal by construction).
- *Judgment:* prefer line/substring granularity for `documentation-model.md` (allowlist only the origin
  note, keep the rest of the file guarded); whole-file allowlisting is acceptable for the tool + test files,
  which contain the literal only as the pattern they scan/test for.

**The test** — `test_check_model_refs.py` under `tests/`, patterned on `tests/test_check_links.py` (pytest,
stubbed). Cover: (a) a fixture with an un-allowlisted `01-conventions` hit → exit 1; (b) the same hit
allowlisted → exit 0; (c) a **real-tree scan** invoking the tool over the actual repo root and asserting
exit 0. Case (c) is the load-bearing one — it puts the consumer-integrity guard on `check_cmd` permanently.

## Test expectations

The independent tester derives tests from the acceptance criteria. `check_cmd` (`pytest tests/ -q`) is
meaningful here: it runs `test_check_model_refs.py`, including the real-tree scan, so a regression (a new
un-allowlisted `01-conventions`, or a broken repoint that leaves a straggler) turns `check_cmd` red. Verify:
the three consumers no longer reference the vault `01-conventions` as the living model; the tool exits 1 on
an un-allowlisted hit and 0 when clean/allowlisted; the real-tree scan of the repo is green.

## Constraints / out of scope

- **Repoint the *living-model* references only.** The allowlisted mentions (origin note, absence-guard test,
  the scanner/its test) are legitimate — allowlist them, do **not** rewrite or delete them.
- **Don't touch the model's content.** `skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md` is authoritative;
  this slice only points at it.
- **Scope wall:** no `SKILL.md` / references changes (that was A2), **no vault writes** (the `01-conventions`
  demote is attended, done after this), and **not** `yellow-robots/.github` (that's Slice C). This is one
  factory-repo PR.

## Size

M — one PR, careful

held_out_tests.content:
import sys
import pathlib
import subprocess

ROOT = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

from tools.check_model_refs import scan, main


def _write(tmp_path, relpath, content):
    p = tmp_path / relpath
    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    p.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8")
    return p


# --- unit cases ---

def test_unallowlisted_hit_exits_1(tmp_path):
    _write(tmp_path, "docs/readme.md",
           "The documentation model is at 01-conventions in the vault.\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 1


def test_allowlisted_line_exits_0(tmp_path):
    _write(tmp_path, "skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md",
           "Relocated from the vault `01-conventions` (iteration), which it supersedes\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 0


def test_clean_tree_exits_0(tmp_path):
    _write(tmp_path, "docs/readme.md", "No forbidden references here.\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 0


def test_full_vault_path_hit_exits_1(tmp_path):
    _write(tmp_path, "docs/note.md",
           "See iterations/3-upper-pipeline/01-conventions.md for the model.\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 1


def test_skip_files_are_ignored(tmp_path):
    # The scanner skips itself and its test — place a forbidden reference there and confirm exit 0
    _write(tmp_path, "tools/check_model_refs.py",
           "# pattern = '01-conventions'\n")
    _write(tmp_path, "tests/test_check_model_refs.py",
           "assert '01-conventions' in output\n")
    _write(tmp_path, "tests/test_skill_factory_router.py",
           "assert '01-conventions' not in text\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 0


def test_skip_vcs_and_build_dirs(tmp_path):
    # Forbidden references inside .git, .venv, node_modules must be ignored
    for skip_dir in (".git", ".venv", "node_modules"):
        _write(tmp_path, f"{skip_dir}/config",
               "01-conventions appears here but is inside a skipped dir\n")
    assert main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)]) == 0


def test_multiple_hits_all_reported(tmp_path, capsys):
    _write(tmp_path, "a.md", "01-conventions line one\n")
    _write(tmp_path, "b.md", "see 01-conventions for details\n")
    result = main(["--scan-root", str(tmp_path)])
    out = capsys.readouterr().out
    assert result == 1
    assert "a.md" in out
    assert "b.md" in out


# --- consumer-repointing checks (AC: no living-model reference to vault 01-conventions) ---

def test_agents_md_has_no_vault_01_conventions():
    """AGENTS.md must not reference vault 01-conventions as the living documentation model."""
    text = (ROOT / "AGENTS.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    bad_lines = [
        line for line in text.splitlines()
        if "01-conventions" in line
    ]
    assert bad_lines == [], (
        f"AGENTS.md still references vault 01-conventions:\n" +
        "\n".join(bad_lines)
    )


def test_agents_md_points_to_skill_documentation_model():
    """AGENTS.md 'documentation model itself is …' passage must point at the skill reference.

    The prose may span two lines (statement on one, path on the next), so we search
    the full text rather than individual lines.
    """
    text = (ROOT / "AGENTS.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "documentation model itself" in text.lower(), (
        "AGENTS.md is missing the 'documentation model itself is …' passage"
    )
    # The path that follows must name the skill reference, not the vault
    assert "documentation-model.md" in text, (
        "AGENTS.md does not reference skills/factory/references/documentation-model.md "
        "near the 'documentation model itself is …' passage"
    )


def test_product_spec_template_has_no_vault_01_conventions():
    """templates/product-spec.md must not reference vault 01-conventions."""
    text = (ROOT / "templates" / "product-spec.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    bad_lines = [
        line for line in text.splitlines()
        if "01-conventions" in line
    ]
    assert bad_lines == [], (
        f"templates/product-spec.md still references vault 01-conventions:\n" +
        "\n".join(bad_lines)
    )


def test_product_spec_review_output_points_to_documentation_model():
    """templates/product-spec.md Review output pointer must name documentation-model."""
    text = (ROOT / "templates" / "product-spec.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    review_lines = [
        line for line in text.splitlines()
        if "Review output" in line or "Reviewing a doc" in line
    ]
    assert review_lines, "templates/product-spec.md is missing a Review output / Reviewing a doc line"
    assert any("documentation-model" in line for line in review_lines), (
        f"templates/product-spec.md Review output pointer does not name documentation-model:\n"
        + "\n".join(review_lines)
    )


def test_feature_rfc_template_has_no_vault_01_conventions():
    """templates/feature-rfc.md must not reference vault 01-conventions."""
    text = (ROOT / "templates" / "feature-rfc.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    bad_lines = [
        line for line in text.splitlines()
        if "01-conventions" in line
    ]
    assert bad_lines == [], (
        f"templates/feature-rfc.md still references vault 01-conventions:\n" +
        "\n".join(bad_lines)
    )


def test_feature_rfc_review_output_points_to_documentation_model():
    """templates/feature-rfc.md Review output pointer must name documentation-model."""
    text = (ROOT / "templates" / "feature-rfc.md").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    review_lines = [
        line for line in text.splitlines()
        if "Review output" in line or "Reviewing a doc" in line
    ]
    assert review_lines, "templates/feature-rfc.md is missing a Review output / Reviewing a doc line"
    assert any("documentation-model" in line for line in review_lines), (
        f"templates/feature-rfc.md Review output pointer does not name documentation-model:\n"
        + "\n".join(review_lines)
    )


# --- real-tree scan (the standing consumer-integrity guard) ---

def test_real_tree_scan_is_green():
    """Scan the actual repo — rides check_cmd on every future build."""
    r = subprocess.run(
        [sys.executable, str(ROOT / "tools" / "check_model_refs.py"),
         "--scan-root", str(ROOT)],
        capture_output=True, text=True,
    )
    assert r.returncode == 0, (
        f"check_model_refs found un-allowlisted 01-conventions references:\n{r.stdout}"
    )
    assert "Traceback" not in (r.stdout + r.stderr)
